title: The Anchor Already Set the Frame
description: Your team is debating a feature's effort. The first guess shaped the rest of the conversation. Name the anchor.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: So we agree it's a two-week effort, give or take. Let's plan around that.

templates.text: Hold on. The two-week number was the first guess in the room. We've been anchoring on it.
templates.hint_zh: 等等,「兩週」是會議裡第一個被丟出來的數字,我們一直被它錨定。

templates.text: Whoever speaks first sets a reference. Every later estimate clusters around it.
templates.hint_zh: 第一個發言的人設了基準,後面的估算都會靠近那個值。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, but two weeks still feels right.

templates.text: Maybe. Try the reverse — independently, write down your own estimate before sharing.
templates.hint_zh: 也許吧。反過來試:大家獨立寫下自己的估算,再公開比較。

templates.text: It de-anchors it. If everyone still says two weeks, great. If not, we have signal.
templates.hint_zh: 這是去錨定。如果大家還是寫兩週,那就確認;不一致的話就有訊號。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Fine. Three minutes, write your number, then we share.

templates.text: Thanks. Worth the three minutes if we're spending two weeks on this.
templates.hint_zh: 謝。要花兩週做的事,先花三分鐘是值得的。
